title: myrsine oblanceolata subsp. oblanceolata
intro: Myrsine oblanceolata subsp. oblanceolata, also known as the Chinese boxwood or Chinese waxleaf, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Myrsine oblanceolata subsp. oblanceolata is often used in teas, infusions, or dried forms, and its leaves can be used to make a paste or oil for topical application.
